channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Takeaways from the Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa deaths investigation
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A towering figure in cinema for decades, retired actor Gene Hackman spent his final years in seclusion with his wife, living with heart disease and Alzheimer's, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alabama minor is jailed with adults pre-trial in violation of federal law
item.description: Activists say an Alabama teenager's incarceration in an adult jail violates federal and state laws that require inmates who are minors to be separated from adults.
item.dc:creator: Safiyah Riddle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Violent and sudden. What a firing squad execution looked like through my eyes
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — I've now watched through glass and bars as 11 men were put to death at a South Carolina prison. None of the previous 10 prepared me for watching the firing squad death of Brad Sigmon on Friday night. I might now be unique among U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Chick-fil-A introduces new spring menu items, brings back fan-favorite sandwich
item.description: Chick-fil-A is celebrating the arrival of spring with new seasonal menu items, including a returning fan favorite.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: A car pulled from a river may tell what happened to an Oregon family of 5 that went missing in 1958
item.description: After two days of dredging, a crane on Friday pulled from the Columbia River a Ford station wagon that officials believe belonged to an Oregon family who disappeared while on a trip 66 years ago to find Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: CDC launches 'conflicts of interest' page about vaccine advisory panel
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) published a page on Friday listing the conflicts of interest reported by members of a key vaccine advisory committee.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: Late car payments hit highest level in decades
item.description: Americans are falling behind on their monthly car payments at the highest rate in more than 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: San Carlos Apache teenager's death reverberates throughout Indian Country
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — From heartbreak and devastation to outrage, Emily Pike's tragic death is stirring heavy emotions and spotlighting a crisis that has long plagued Native American communities, where a disproportionate number of people have been killed or have gone missing.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration throws hundreds of affordable housing projects into limbo after contract cuts
item.description: The Trump administration has stalled at least $60 million in funding intended largely for affordable housing developments nationwide, throwing hundreds of projects into a precarious limbo, according to information and documents obtained by The Associated Press The move is part of a flurry of funding
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A South Carolina man executed by firing squad is the first US prisoner killed this way in 15 years
item.description: A South Carolina man has been executed by firing squad, becoming the first U.S. prisoner in 15 years to die by this method.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: California, other states suing Trump administration over mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: California and 19 other states are suing multiple federal agencies, contending President Donald Trump's administration has illegally fired thousands of federal probationary workers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump threatens tariffs on Canadian dairy, lumber products as soon as Friday
item.description: Access to dairy and lumber markets have long been two of the biggest points of contention in the otherwise strong economic relationship between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Elementary student found unharmed after escaping school
item.description: " I just jumped out of the car and tried to grab him to make sure that there was nobody that actually would hit him," Kohler said.
item.dc:creator: Cheyenne Pagan, NewsNation

item.title: Cheetos bags full of meth found at Indiana post office
item.description: Two Los Angeles residents have been arrested in southern Indiana after police found pounds of meth hidden inside Cheetos bags at a local post office.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, KRON4

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs fans' cause of death revealed after being found frozen in friend's yard
item.description: The three Kansas City Chiefs fans found frozen in their friend's backyard died of fentanyl and cocaine toxicity, as new details about their bizarre deaths unravel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: War heroes, a bomber and military firsts among 26,000 images flagged for removal in Pentagon's DEI purge
item.description: Photos seemed to be flagged for removal simply because their file included the word ”gay,” including an image of the B-29 aircraft Enola Gay, which dropped the first atomic bomb on Hiroshima, Japan, during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Florida residents record SpaceX explosion
item.description: Residents in Tampa Bay, Florida were confounded after seeing what seemed to be an explosion in the sky around Thursday's sunset.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Second measles death reported as outbreak grows in Southwest
item.description: The person was unvaccinated and a resident of Lea County, New Mexico, where at least 10 cases of the disease have been reported.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's move to restore old Army base names highlights Nebraska man's heroics at 18 in World War I
item.description: For more than a century, Fort Benning’s name honored a Confederate general who supported slavery. The military changed the name of the Army base in Georgia two years ago, but now the Trump administration is set on restoring the familiar one — this time for a different Benning.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he will 'probably' extend TikTok ban deadline if deal isn't reached in time
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested he would consider extending the deadline for a ban on TikTok for a second time if a deal isn't reached, stating there is "a lot of interest" in the video-sharing platform.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KTLA 5

item.title: Southwest plane forced to go back to gate as passenger strips naked: reports
item.description: A Southwest passenger forced a plane bound for Phoenix from Houston this week to return to the gate after she took off all of her clothes and started harassing others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Men accused of stealing and reselling StubHub tickets for $635K: DA
item.description: Two men accused of stealing and reselling hundreds of StubHub tickets were arrested and arraigned Monday, according to Queens District Attorney Melinda Katz.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No prison time for man who bashed, disfigured mother’s face in front of child
item.description: Trevor Lee Colombano, 38, faced up to nine years in state prison in addition to 18 months in county jail after pleading no contest to assault with a deadly weapon, sexual battery and mayhem.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, NewsNation

item.title: Cockpit recorder likely hadn't worked 'for several years' before jet crashed in Philadelphia: NTSB
item.description: The cockpit voice recorder in the medical transport jet that slammed into a Philadelphia neighborhood in January had likely not worked for years, according to a preliminary report released by the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: CDC rehiring some laid-off workers
item.description: Administration officials told the CDC last month that nearly 1,300 of its probationary employees would be cut, but only approximately 700 to 750 employees ended up receiving termination notices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Police identify 2 people who died in Feb. 19 midair collision at southern Arizona airport
item.description: MARANA, Ariz. (AP) — Authorities have publicly identified two people killed two weeks ago in the midair collision of two small planes at a southern Arizona airport. Police say 70-year-old Michael Reinath and 76-year-old Linda Gifford died in the Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: Veterans fired from federal jobs say they feel betrayed, including some who voted for Trump
item.description: Nathan Hooven is a disabled Air Force veteran who voted for Donald Trump in November. Barely three months later, he's now unemployed and says he feels betrayed by the president’s dramatic downsizing of the federal government that cost him his job.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Scientists raise concerns as the US stops sharing air quality data from embassies worldwide
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The U.S. government will stop sharing air quality data gathered from its embassies and consulates, worrying local scientists and experts who say the effort was vital to monitor global air quality and improve public health.
item.dc:creator: TAIWO ADEBAYO and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Babysitter accused of drowning child during bathtub baptism
item.description: A Muncie woman was babysitting a close friend's children at her home in Texas when she allegedly decided to baptize the children in the tub.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, WFLA, NBC
